data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series G defense and security technology company, carries a last post-money valuation of $7.5B following $1.4B+ in cumulative fundraising. The peer universe comprises 9 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, spanning 2 public equities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Shield AI ($12.7B, Series G), Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), and Saronic ($9.2B, Series D).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.25x multiplier typical of Series G advancement, yielding $9.4B. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company Defense & Security cohort—with a range of $2.1B to $18.0B—implies $4.5B, substantially below the current valuation. The disparity reflects both valuation dispersion in frontier defense tech and potential company-specific positioning above median peers.

The triangulated range spans $3.8B (low) to $10.8B (high), with a median of $6.9B. The current $7.5B valuation sits near the median, supported primarily by stage-progression logic but challenged by peer positioning. Key uncertainties include whether Flock Safety commands meaningful multiples above the peer median and the sustainability of Defense & Security valuations in a shifting venture and public market environment. Further diligence on revenue, growth trajectory, and market share relative to Shield AI and Helsing would refine this range.
